title: Garlicky Korean Chicken Stir-fry
description: This quick-fix Venezuelan dish features tender chicken cooked in a low-calorie-friendly stock with garlic and other aromatic herbs for a vibrant kick.

ingredients.name: Korean red pepper flakes (gochugaru)
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste for heat level

instructions: Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside. In the same pan, add minced garlic, Korean red pepper flakes, and a few drops of olive oil if desired. Sauté for another minute or so until fragrant.
Add the chicken stock to the pan and stir to combine with the aromatics. Bring to a simmer and let it cook for about 3 minutes.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss well to coat evenly in the liquid, letting the flavors meld together.
Stir in sugar until dissolved, and squeeze in the lime juice if using. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Spoon the stir-fry onto a plate, garnish with chopped scallions, and serve immediately.
